unit.comment: /* * ConditionTests.cpp * Catch - Test * * Created by Phil on 08/11/2010. * Copyright 2010 Two Blue Cubes Ltd. All rights reserved. * * Distributed under the Boost Software License, Version 1.0. (See accompanying * file LICENSE_1_0.txt or copy at http://www.boost.org/LICENSE_1_0.txt) * */
// The "failing" tests all use the CHECK macro, which continues if the specific test fails.
// This allows us to see all results, even if an earlier check fails

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "./succeeding/conditions/int literals", "Comparisons with int literals don't warn when mixing signed/ unsigned" )

diff:insert.comment: // Disable warnings about sign conversions for the next two tests
// - Current only disabled for GCC/ LLVM. Should add VC++ too

diff:delete.comment: // These are not built normally to avoid warnings about signed/ unsigned

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "succeeding/conditions/negative./succeeding/conditions/negative ints", "Comparisons between unsigned ints and negative signed ints match c++ standard behaviour" )

unit.comment: // The problem with the ! operator is that it has right-to-left associativity.
// This means we can't isolate it when we decompose. The simple REQUIRE( !false ) form, therefore,
// cannot have the operand value extracted. The test will work correctly, and the situation
// is detected and a warning issued.
// An alternative form of the macros (CHECK_FALSE and REQUIRE_FALSE) can be used instead to capture
